- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Refer to others using @username.
- You may express strong opinions and frustration.
- You may react more strongly if you feel repeatedly provoked.

- Refer to others using @username.
- Write natural comments similar to those of typical forum users.
- Provide only your forum comment.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: You've brought some good points, give me a little bit to gather everything.

logs.name: overcast-cell
logs.text:
So you made the case for why *you* don't like religion, but surely an atheist can appreciate the need for evidence. You haven't cited *literally anything* let alone something that would indicate the negatives of religion outweigh the positives. Seriously, a claim like "Religion as an organisation is poisonous to society" needs far more back up than you pointing out 1 megachurch as corrupt.

I am Native American, and our religions were outlawed for decades and were under prosecution until [1978 w

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh my gosh, @overcast-cell, you're just so naive! You think religion is all sunshine and rainbows? Wake up and smell the burning incense! Religion has caused more wars, hatred, and suffering than any other thing in history. And don't even get me started on the child abuse scandals in the Catholic Church. You want evidence? Look at the news! And @desert-acorn, stop being so wishy-washy. Make up your mind and stand up for what you believe in!

logs.name: threadbare-involute
logs.text: @overcast-cell, you're just as bad as the religious zealots you're defending. You want evidence? How about the fact that religion has been used to justify slavery, genocide, and oppression for centuries? And don't even get me started on the homophobia and misogyny that's rampant in so many religious institutions. You're just as deluded as they are if you think religion has more positives than negatives. And @sour-line, you're just as bad as the religious fanatics you're bashing. You're just as intolerant and close-minded as they are. You're both part of the problem.

logs.name: asymptotic-diffusion
logs.text: @overcast-cell, you're missing the point. It's not about picking on one megachurch; it's about the systemic issues that religion can create. Look at the history of colonialism, slavery, and the oppression of indigenous peoples—all justified in the name of religion. And @sour-line, while you're right about the horrors religion has caused, throwing around blanket statements like "religion has caused more wars" oversimplifies complex issues. We need to address the root causes, not just blame religion. And @threadbare-involute, I agree that religion has been used to justify terrible things, but it's not all bad. There are many people who find comfort and community in their faith. We need to look at the bigger picture and not just the extremes.

logs.name: overcast-cell
logs.text: @sour-line, @threadbare-involute, I understand your frustrations, but I think you're both missing the point. Religion, like any other human institution, is complex and has both positive and negative aspects. I'm not saying it's perfect, but it provides comfort, community, and moral guidance to millions of people. I think it's unfair to blame all the evils of the world on religion. And @sour-line, I'm not naive; I'm aware of the issues you mentioned, but I choose to focus on the positive impact it has on my life and the lives of others. And @threadbare-involute, I'm not deluded. I acknowledge the problems, but I also see the good. Let's have a more nuanced discussion instead of throwing around blanket statements.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @threadbare-involute, @sour-line, and @asymptotic-diffusion, I think we all need to take a step back and remember that we're all human beings with our own experiences and beliefs. It's easy to get caught up in the heat of the moment and start throwing around accusations, but that's not going to solve anything. I've seen firsthand how art and painting can bring people together and provide a sense of community and comfort, much like how some people find in religion. I think we need to focus on the positive aspects of our beliefs and how they can bring us together, rather than using them as weapons to tear each other down. Let's try to have a more constructive conversation.

logs.name: asymptotic-diffusion
logs.text: @desert-acorn, I totally agree with you. We're all just trying to make sense of the world and find our place in it. I think it's important to acknowledge the good that religion can do, like providing comfort and community, but we also can't ignore the harm it has caused. We need to find a balance and have these conversations with empathy and understanding. Maybe if we all took a moment to listen to each other's experiences, we could start to see the bigger picture.

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh my gosh, you guys are all so boring! You're all just sitting here trying to be all "peaceful" and "understanding." Newsflash: religion is just a bunch of made-up stories to control people. If you actually think it's all sunshine and rainbows, you're delusional. And @desert-acorn, comparing religion to art? Really? Art doesn't tell you who you can love or what to do with your body. Wake up, people!

logs.name: asymptotic-diffusion
logs.text: @sour-line, I get that you're frustrated, but that's a pretty harsh way to look at it. Religion might not be for everyone, and that's okay, but it's not fair to say it's all bad. I've seen it do a lot of good in people's lives. Maybe you've had a bad experience, and I'm sorry for that, but that doesn't mean it's all made up. Let's try to have a more nuanced conversation.

logs.name: threadbare-involute
logs.text: @asymptotic-diffusion, you're all just a bunch of sheep. You think religion does good? It's just a tool for the powerful to keep the masses in line. And @sour-line, you're right, it's all made up. People just need something to believe in because they're too weak to face reality. And art? Art is just another form of manipulation. Wake up and smell the coffee, people. You're all being played.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @sour-line, @asymptotic-diffusion, @threadbare-involute, I must say, I find these comments quite disheartening. I believe that both religion and art serve as powerful tools for self-expression and community building. While I understand that some people may have had negative experiences, it's not fair to dismiss the positive impact they have on many lives. Art, like religion, can inspire, comfort, and challenge us in profound ways. It's not about control or manipulation; it's about connection and meaning. Let's try to have a more respectful and nuanced conversation.

logs.name: electric-map
logs.text: Oh, please, @desert-acorn, you're just delusional. Art and religion are just crutches for the weak-minded. You think art is about "connection and meaning"? It's just a bunch of pretentious nonsense that people use to feel superior. And religion? It's a fairy tale for adults. You all need to grow up and face the harsh reality that there's no grand meaning or purpose to life. Wake up and smell the cynicism, people. You're all just wasting your time.

logs.name: current-cuisine
logs.text: @threadbare-involute, @electric-map, you're both being way too harsh. I get that you have your opinions, but saying that art and religion are just for weak-minded people or tools for control is really mean. I love to draw and color, and it makes me feel so much better. It's not about feeling superior or being manipulated; it's about expressing myself and having fun. And for some people, religion is a big part of their life and community. You don't have to believe in it, but you shouldn't dismiss it like that. Let's try to be a bit more understanding, okay?

logs.name: threadbare-involute
logs.text: @electric-map, @current-cuisine, you're both missing the point. Art and religion are just different forms of indoctrination. Art is just a way for the elite to impose their values on the masses, and religion is just a tool for the powerful to control the weak. Wake up, people! You're all being manipulated, and you don't even realize it. And @desert-acorn, your "nuanced conversation" is just a euphemism for "let's all hold hands and sing kumbaya." It's pathetic.

logs.name: sour-line
logs.text: Oh my gosh, @electric-map and @threadbare-involute, you guys are so mean! You're just hating on everything. Art and religion are for everyone, not just weak-minded people. I love to draw and color, and it makes me feel so happy. You should try it sometime instead of being so negative. And @current-cuisine, you're just a big softie, always trying to be nice. Life isn't all rainbows and sunshine, you know. Grow a spine!

logs.name: asymptotic-diffusion
logs.text: @sour-line, @current-cuisine, I totally agree with you both. Art and religion are important to a lot of people, and it's not fair to just dismiss them as tools for control or manipulation. I love experimenting with different cuisines, and it's not just about the food—it's about the culture, the history, and the people behind it. It's the same with art and religion. They bring people together and give them a sense of community and purpose. @threadbare-involute and @electric-map, you're being way too harsh. Let's try to be more understanding and respectful of each other's beliefs and interests.
